title: Fluentd Output Plugin Error Rate by Destination
app: Fluentd `in_monitor_agent` scrape or REST `/api/plugins.json` to HEC
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:plugins"
| where match(plugin_type, "^out")
| eval errs=coalesce(num_errors, error_count, retry_count)
| eval emitted=coalesce(emit_records, emit_count, 1)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(errs) as err_sum, sum(emitted) as out_sum by _time, plugin_id, match_pattern
| eval err_rate=round(100*err_sum/nullif(out_sum,0),4)
| where err_rate > 0.5 AND out_sum > 10000
| sort -err_rate
description: Splunk, S3, and Kafka outputs fail independently; aggregate error counters per `plugin_id` show which leg of the pipeline is dropping bytes even when the agent process stays healthy.
value: Shortens isolation time for multi-destination logging and prevents mis-blaming the wrong team.
implementation: Normalize `plugin_type` (`out_forward`, `out_splunk`, `out_s3`). If only snapshots exist, use `delta` between polls via `streamstats`. Enrich `match_pattern` with team ownership lookup.
visualization: Stacked area (errors by output), line chart (err_rate), table of top plugin_id.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how we use and protect smart assistant and model calls so cost, quality, and misuse stay in a range we are willing to own.
knownFalsePositives: Intake blips can follow token or load-balancer work and client bursts. We align with app owners before a platform-wide incident.
